hero.title: Measure real developer <productivity>productivity</productivity>
hero.description: Analyze Git repositories with AI-powered effort estimation. Get Ghost % — the ratio of actual productive output to expected work capacity.
hero.ctaDescription: Create an account and start analyzing repositories today.

aiEffort.title: AI Effort Estimation
aiEffort.description: Each commit is analyzed by an LLM to estimate real cognitive effort in hours — not just lines of code.

ghostMetric.title: Ghost % Metric
ghostMetric.description: Compare estimated effort against standard work capacity. See who delivers above or below the norm.

teamAnalytics.title: Team Analytics
teamAnalytics.description: Aggregate metrics per developer, per period. Identify productivity patterns and anomalies.

addRepos.title: Add repositories
addRepos.description: Connect your GitHub account or add public repos by URL. DevGhost extracts commit history automatically.

runAnalysis.title: Run analysis
runAnalysis.description: AI estimates cognitive effort for each commit — accounting for complexity, not just diff size.

reviewGhost.title: Review Ghost %
reviewGhost.description: See each developer's Ghost % — the ratio of estimated productive output to expected capacity.

login.title: Sign in
login.description: Enter your email and password to access your account
login.registeredSuccess: Account created successfully. Please sign in.
login.errorGeneric: An error occurred. Please try again.

register.title: Create account
register.description: Enter your email to create your account
register.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
register.errorGeneric: An error occurred. Please try again.

metadata.title: DevGhost — Ghost % Analytics
metadata.description: Measure developer productivity with AI-powered effort estimation and Ghost % metrics
metadata.keywords: developer analytics, ghost percent, productivity metrics, AI effort estimation, Git analysis, team analytics

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.description: Create your first code analysis to get developer productivity metrics
getStarted.step1: Select repositories from your GitHub account

help.what: This screen gives you a high-level overview of teams, contributors, repositories, and freshness for the current scope.
help.how: Use the global scope bar first, then scan highlights and freshness to decide where to drill down next.

highlights.title: Highlights
highlights.description: The most relevant teams, contributors, and repositories in the current scope

freshness.title: Freshness
freshness.description: How fresh the visible repository set is

saveState.saved: This scope matches the active saved view.
saveState.dirty: This scope differs from the active saved view.
saveState.adhoc: This is an ad hoc scope. Save it if you want to reuse it.

empty.title: Welcome to DevGhost
empty.description: Start by analyzing a repository to see your engineering activity.
empty.step1: Run your first analysis to import commits and contributors
empty.step2: Review contributors and repositories to confirm identity
empty.step3: Create your first team to unlock the full dashboard

firstData.title: Your workspace has data
firstData.description: Review your contributors and repositories, then create your first team to unlock the full dashboard.
firstData.teamCtaTitle: Ready to create your first team?
firstData.teamHint: Start from your most active repository — the system will suggest team members from its contributors.

firstSavedView.title: Save your first reusable view
firstSavedView.description: Open your team, choose a useful date range, and save it as a report you can reopen anytime.

saveView.title: Save current scope
saveView.description: Store this scope as a reusable saved view.
saveView.name: Name

reports.title: Reports
reports.description: Saved views you can reopen and reuse

help.what: This screen lists saved views that capture reusable scopes for teams, repositories, contributors, and dates.
help.how: Search for a saved view, open it to inspect details, or activate it to apply that scope across analytics screens.

reports.empty: No saved views yet. Save a scope from the dashboard to create your first report.
reports.filteredEmpty: No saved views match the current filters.
reports.scopeKind: {kind, select, all_teams {All teams} team {Single team} custom {Custom scope} other {Scope}}

reportDetail.description: Inspect and manage this saved view

help.title: Saved View
help.what: This screen shows exactly what a saved view contains: scope, filters, visibility, and whether your current URL-backed scope matches it.
help.how: Use it to verify the saved scope, update its name or visibility, and activate it when you want to work inside that saved context.

reportDetail.notFound: This saved view does not exist or you do not have access to it.

scope.title: Resolved Scope
scope.dirty: Current URL-backed scope differs from this saved view.
scope.synced: Current URL-backed scope matches this saved view.

orders.title: Analyses
orders.noOrdersDescription: Run your first code analysis to get developer productivity metrics
orders.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this analysis?

onboardingHandoff.title: What's next?
onboardingHandoff.description: Your analysis is complete. Here's what to do with the results:

contributorSelector.title: Contributors
contributorSelector.subtitle: Select which contributors to include in the analysis. Deselected contributors will be excluded from results and billing.

identityReview.banner: {count, plural, one {# contributor needs} other {# contributors need}} identity review

detail.ghostNormMedianFallback: Not enough data for median. Using fixed Ghost Norm {hours} h/day.
detail.fetchingCommits: Fetching commits from {count, plural, one {# repository} other {# repositories}}
detail.preparingExtract: {count, plural, one {# repository} other {# repositories}} selected. Preparing to extract developers from commits...
detail.zeroBillableCommits: All commits cached — no credits needed
detail.firstRunEstimateHint: Estimate based on extraction data — may be conservative for date-scoped runs
detail.adminQuickTopUpHint: Quick self top-up without leaving this page.
detail.adminCreditBypassHint: Admin mode: you can start analysis even with insufficient credits.
detail.analysisPausedDescription: The analysis could not proceed because your credit balance is too low.
detail.pendingStaleHint: Job is still pending for over 2 minutes. Usually this means Modal trigger failed and watchdog should re-trigger it.
detail.heartbeatStaleHint: Heartbeat is delayed. Worker may be stuck on a long step (clone/LLM) or connection is unstable.
detail.heartbeatCriticalHint: No heartbeat for over 10 minutes. Job is likely stuck; watchdog should mark it retryable/fatal soon.
detail.postProcessingStaleHint: LLM phase is done but post-processing is taking too long. Check watchdog runs and server logs.
detail.noLiveEventsHint: No runtime events yet. They will appear as each stage advances.
detail.noLiveLogHint: No commit-level live log yet. In modal mode this is expected until results are persisted.
detail.processedLocally: Processed locally via Ollama ({model}, {calls} calls, {tokens} tokens)
detail.modeChangeWarning: This analysis uses Selected Years mode ({years}). Saving will change the mode to the one selected below.

commitsTab.author: Author
commitsTab.effortSpreadSummary: {effort} across {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}

new.title: New Analysis

help.what: Use this screen to choose which repositories go into one analysis run.
help.how: Public repositories can be analyzed right away through Public Repository or Explore. GitHub connection is only needed for your own and private repositories in My Repositories.

new.analysisNameDescription: Give your analysis a descriptive name
new.analysisNamePlaceholder: Leave empty to auto-generate from selected repositories
new.selectReposDescription: Choose the repositories you want to analyze
new.publicAccessTitle: Analyze public repositories without connecting GitHub
new.publicAccessDescription: Use Public Repository or Explore to analyze any public repo right away.
new.privateAccessTitle: Connect GitHub for your own and private repositories
new.privateAccessDescription: My Repositories becomes available after connecting GitHub.
new.reposSelected: {count, plural, one {# repository} other {# repositories}} selected

handoff.title: Your analysis is complete. Here's what to do next.
handoff.description: Review the imported data, then create your first team.
handoff.teamFallbackCta: Find a repository to start your first team

returnBanner.text: You came here from analysis results.

billing.title: Billing
billing.description: Manage your credits, subscriptions, and payment methods
billing.noSubscription: No active subscription. Subscribe below to get monthly credits at a discount.
billing.noPacks: No credit packs available at the moment.
billing.subscriptionPlansDescription: Monthly subscriptions with bonus credits. Cancel anytime.
billing.noPlans: No subscription plans available at the moment.
billing.promoCodeDescription: Have a promo code? Redeem it for free credits.
billing.freeModeDescription: All analyses are free — no credits required.
billing.referralDescription: Invite friends and earn credits for each signup.
billing.cancelDialogDescription: Your subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing period. Subscription credits will expire at that time. This action cannot be undone.
billing.subscriptionCancelledDescription: Your subscription will end at the current billing period.

settings.title: Settings
settings.githubDescription: Connect your GitHub account to access repositories
settings.pipelineCacheDescription: Cached git diffs and LLM responses used to speed up benchmark re-runs

publications.title: Publications
publications.description: Manage your published analytics. Share links with clients and colleagues.
publications.noPublications: No publications yet. Publish an analysis from the analysis detail page.
publications.noPublicationsSubtitle: Publish analytics from a completed analysis to share them publicly.
publications.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete the publication for {slug}? The share link will stop working immediately.

profile.title: Developer Profile
profile.createDescription: Create a public developer profile to showcase your analytics.
profile.slugHint: 3-30 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
profile.visibleHint: When disabled, your profile page will return 404.

demo.title: DevGhost Demo
demo.description: Create a demo analysis with sample Git commits and Ghost % metrics
demo.intro: This will create a completed analysis with:

explore.title: Explore
explore.description: Discover developer productivity analytics for open source repositories.
explore.publishCtaDescription: Share your repository analytics with the community.
explore.noMetrics: No metrics data available for this repository.

kpi.singleDevWarning: Single developer — Ghost % reflects absolute effort vs {norm}h/day norm, not relative productivity.

analysisPeriod.title: Analysis Period
analysisPeriod.description: Choose which commits to include in the analysis
analysisPeriod.allTimeDescription: Analyze all commits from repository history
analysisPeriod.dateRangeDescription: Select a specific date range to analyze
analysisPeriod.lastNDescription: Analyze a fixed number of recent commits
analysisPeriod.loadingDateRange: Loading available date range from repositories...
analysisPeriod.invalidRange: Start date must be before end date

users.title: User Management
users.name: Name
users.deleteUserDescription: This will permanently delete {email} and all their orders. This action cannot be undone.
users.amountNonZero: Amount must be a non-zero integer
users.passwordResetDescription: Temporary password for {email}. Share it securely — it won't be shown again.
users.adjustCreditsDescription: Adjust permanent credits for {email}. Current balance: {total} credits ({permanent} permanent + {subscription} subscription).

orders.title: All Orders
orders.description: View and manage all system orders
orders.name: Name
orders.deleteOrderDescription: This will permanently delete "{name}" (owned by {email}). This action cannot be undone.

billing.title: Billing Overview
billing.creditsSoldDetail: From pack purchases and subscription renewals
billing.creditsInCirculationDetail: Sum of all users' permanent + subscription credits

promo.title: Promo Codes
promo.createPromoDescription: Create a new promotional code that users can redeem for credits.
promo.deletePromoDescription: This will delete the promo code "{code}".
promo.deletePromoRedeemed: Since it has been redeemed, it will be deactivated instead of deleted.
promo.fieldsRequired: Code, credits, and expiration date are required

settings.title: System Settings
settings.description: Configure LLM provider and manage pipeline cache
settings.llmProviderDescription: Configure the default AI model used for small and normal commit analysis (up to 49 files)
settings.demoLiveModeHint: For admin pages, poll analysis status much more frequently for near real-time updates during demos.
settings.demoLiveChunkSizeHint: How many commits to process per LLM chunk in demo live mode (1-200).
settings.keyFromEnv: Using key from OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var. Enter a new key to override.
settings.keyFromDb: Key is saved in database. Clear and save to fall back to env var.
settings.failedLoadModels: Failed to load models. You can type a model ID manually below.
settings.providerOrderHint: Try these providers first in this exact order.
settings.allowFallbacksHint: If first provider fails, try others automatically.
settings.requireParametersHint: Route only to providers supporting all request options.
settings.pipelineCacheDescription: Cached git diffs and LLM responses used to speed up benchmark re-runs
settings.failedFetchPricing: Failed to fetch pricing from OpenRouter
settings.largePathDescription: Separate model for commits with 50+ files. Configured via environment variables, not editable here.
settings.largePathEnvNote: Set via FD_V3_ENABLED, FD_LARGE_LLM_PROVIDER, FD_LARGE_LLM_MODEL environment variables
settings.fallbackRateDescription: Recent production commit analyses that landed exactly on 5.0h.
settings.fdShareDescription: How many recent FD routes reached the new FD v3 holistic estimator path.
settings.modalJobsDescription: Current stuck jobs plus failed modal analyses in the last 24 hours.
settings.modalJobsHint: {failed} failed, {pending} pending stale, {running} running stale, {post} llm-complete stale
settings.attributionDescription: Obvious per-commit attribution mistakes in recent production analyses.
settings.attributionHint: {heuristic} heuristic mislabeled, {special} special mislabeled, {missing} large-model missing

monitoring.title: System Monitoring
monitoring.description: Active jobs, cache, and deep pipeline health checks
monitoring.pipelineHealthDescription: Environment validation, trigger reachability, watchdog freshness, and per-job atomic step tracking.
monitoring.stuckJobs: {count, plural, one {# stuck job} other {# stuck jobs}}
monitoring.jobsRunning: {count, plural, one {# job running} other {# jobs running}}
monitoring.pipelineCacheDescription: Cached git repos, diffs, and LLM responses
monitoring.schedulerDescription: Built-in Vercel Cron calls watchdog every minute to process stuck and completed jobs.

publications.title: Publications
publications.description: Curate and manage all published analytics
publications.createPublicationDescription: Select a completed order and repository to publish.
publications.noReposForOrder: No repos found for this order
publications.deletePublicationDescription: Are you sure you want to delete the publication for {slug}? This action cannot be undone.

help.what: This screen shows the canonical contributor catalog for the current scope, including identity health and exclusions.
help.how: Use filters to narrow the table, review the identity queue for unresolved aliases, and open contributor detail when you need to inspect one person.

empty.title: No contributors yet
empty.description: Run an analysis to populate contributors.

help.title: Contributor Detail
help.what: This screen shows one contributor across aliases, repositories, and commit evidence so you can understand their canonical identity and activity.
help.how: Review aliases first, then inspect repository breakdown and commit evidence before merging, classifying, or excluding the contributor.

help.what: This screen is the repository catalog for the current scope, with freshness, contributor counts, and activity summaries.
help.how: Use search and filters to find the repository you care about, then open detail to inspect contributors and recent activity.

empty.title: No repositories yet
empty.description: Run an analysis to populate your repository catalog.

firstTeamBanner.title: Ready to create your first team?
firstTeamBanner.description: Open a repository you know well and use "Create Team from Repository" to bootstrap your first team from its contributors.

help.title: Repository Detail
help.what: This screen explains one canonical repository through activity, contributors, and recent commits in the current scope.
help.how: Use it to see who is active in the repository, what work is recent, and whether unresolved contributors still need identity cleanup.

createTeam.title: Create Team from Repository
createTeam.name: Team name
createTeam.recentHint: Preselected from the most recent repository activity.
createTeam.emptyContributors: No canonical contributors found for this repository.
createTeam.error: Failed to create team from repository

help.what: This screen lists canonical teams and summarizes their member counts, repository activity, and recent work.
help.how: Use it to discover the team you want, sort by recent activity or size, and open team detail to manage membership and review team-level output.

empty.title: No teams yet
empty.description: Start from a repository to bootstrap your first team, then refine it as a durable management scope.

create.title: Create Team
create.name: Team name
create.descriptionPlaceholder: What does this team work on?
create.duplicate: A team with this name already exists

help.title: Team Detail
help.what: This screen is the management view for a single team, combining membership history with repositories derived from team activity.
help.how: Use it to review who belongs to the team, adjust memberships when needed, and check which repositories the team was active in for the selected period.

members.title: Team Members
members.name: Name
members.removeConfirm: Remove this member from the team?

repositories.title: Active Repositories
repositories.description: Repositories where team members have committed in the selected period.
repositories.name: Repository
repositories.empty: No repository activity found for this team

onboarding.title: Your first team is ready
onboarding.description: Review the suggested members and repositories below. Then choose a useful date range in the top scope bar and click Save View to create your first reusable report.
onboarding.saveViewTitle: Save this as your first report
onboarding.saveViewDescription: Choose a useful date range in the scope bar above, then save this view so you can return to it anytime.

addMember.title: Add Team Member
addMember.emptyCandidates: No contributors match the current filters.
addMember.suggestedStartDate: Start date will be set to first known activity: {date}
addMember.alreadyMember: This contributor is already a member

settings.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This will remove the team and all memberships.
